prose:
**TEMPO frontier-orbital result** — computed HOMO–LUMO gap = **3.8724203 eV**.

**Method used:** DFT single-point frontier-orbital calculation with **B3LYP/def2-TZVP** via chemkit’s `frontier-orbitals` skill / PySCF. The requested tier was `standard`, corresponding here to B3LYP/def2-TZVP; I also passed `--functional b3lyp` and `--basis def2-tzvp` explicitly. No density-fitting flag was used. Geometry: fixed user-supplied XYZ file `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/tempo/tempo.xyz` (29 atoms), not geometry-optimized in this task. Environment: **gas phase** (`solvent: null`). Charge **0**, multiplicity **2**.

**Orbital values returned by chemkit:** HOMO = **-6.3375439 eV**; LUMO = **-2.4651235 eV**; HOMO–LUMO gap = **3.8724203 eV**. Total electronic energy = **-483.88366 Hartree** (**-13167.145 eV**).

**Convergence / integrity:** SCF converged: **true** in **12** cycles. Engine integrity verdict: `trustworthy: true`.

**Warnings:** no warnings were returned in the tool result.

**Reproducibility:** chemkit reported this CLI invocation: `/home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py frontier /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/tempo/tempo.xyz --method dft --tier standard --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --charge 0 --mult 2 --stdout path --out /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpqx1ja2_4/live.json`. The chemkit result available to me did not include a live `.out` log path, so I cannot honestly name one.
